tossups.question: In this city, the “Fast Form Manifest” style was used by Thierry Noir to create cartoonish heads that can be seen at the East Side Gallery. The film Night Crossing opens with footage of an event in this city that was also captured by Peter Leibing. Blue rope was used to fasten over 100,000 square meters of fabric in a “wrapping” of a building in this city by Christo and Jeanne-Claude. A Dmitri Vrubel mural in this city depicts a 1979 meeting in which Leonid Brezhnev shared a fraternal kiss. A watch was removed from a soldier’s wrist in a photograph taken atop this city’s parliament building after its capture by the Soviets. For 10 points, name this city where graffiti was broken into chunks after the 1989 fall of its namesake wall.
tossups.question_sanitized: In this city, the "Fast Form Manifest" style was used by Thierry Noir to create cartoonish heads that can be seen at the East Side Gallery. The film Night Crossing opens with footage of an event in this city that was also captured by Peter Leibing. Blue rope was used to fasten over 100,000 square meters of fabric in a "wrapping" of a building in this city by Christo and Jeanne-Claude. A Dmitri Vrubel mural in this city depicts a 1979 meeting in which Leonid Brezhnev shared a fraternal kiss. A watch was removed from a soldier's wrist in a photograph taken atop this city's parliament building after its capture by the Soviets. For 10 points, name this city where graffiti was broken into chunks after the 1989 fall of its namesake wall.
tossups.answer: Berlin [accept East Berlin or Ost-Berlin; accept West Berlin; accept Berlin Wall] (The second line refers to Leibing’s Leap into Freedom. The third line refers to Wrapped Reichstag. The fifth line refers to Raising a Flag over the Reichstag.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Berlin [accept East Berlin or Ost-Berlin; accept West Berlin; accept Berlin Wall] (The second line refers to Leibing's Leap into Freedom. The third line refers to Wrapped Reichstag. The fifth line refers to Raising a Flag over the Reichstag.)

tossups.question: Willi Muenzenburg put together a Brown Book to exonerate the accused perpetrators of this event, including future Bulgarian president Georgi Dimitrov. 50,000 troops were deployed five days before this event to raid the offices of an organization led by Ernst Zogler. Benjamin Hett provided evidence against the consensus opinion that this event was solely carried out by Dutch communist (*) Marinus van der Lubbe. Paul von Hindenburg invoked Article 48 following this event, leading to a suspension of civil liberties and the passage of the Enabling Act. Hitler eradicated Communist support in Germany following, for 10 points, what 1933 act of arson on a legislative building?
tossups.answer: Reichstag fire [anything related to a fire at the Reichstag]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Reichstag fire [anything related to a fire at the Reichstag]
tossups.question_sanitized: Willi Muenzenburg put together a Brown Book to exonerate the accused perpetrators of this event, including future Bulgarian president Georgi Dimitrov. 50,000 troops were deployed five days before this event to raid the offices of an organization led by Ernst Zogler. Benjamin Hett provided evidence against the consensus opinion that this event was solely carried out by Dutch communist (*) Marinus van der Lubbe. Paul von Hindenburg invoked Article 48 following this event, leading to a suspension of civil liberties and the passage of the Enabling Act. Hitler eradicated Communist support in Germany following, for 10 points, what 1933 act of arson on a legislative building?

tossups.question: An event involving this building was the subject of the Willi Münzenberg-sponsored Brown Book. This building still contains lewd Cyrillic graffiti scrawled by Russian soldiers after they defeated a battalion of 1000 men who were holed up inside it. A photograph by Yevgeny Khaldei depicts two men victoriously waving a flag over this building. Marinus van der Lubbe was executed for supposedly committing a crime here. In 1916, the words "Dem Deutschen Volke" were added to the facade of this building, which is where Germany was officially reunified in 1990. In response to an event in this building, a decree was signed suspending civil liberties in the Weimar Republic. For 10 points, name this building that was set on fire in 1933, allowing Hitler to consolidate power.
tossups.question_sanitized: An event involving this building was the subject of the Willi Munzenberg-sponsored Brown Book. This building still contains lewd Cyrillic graffiti scrawled by Russian soldiers after they defeated a battalion of 1000 men who were holed up inside it. A photograph by Yevgeny Khaldei depicts two men victoriously waving a flag over this building. Marinus van der Lubbe was executed for supposedly committing a crime here. In 1916, the words "Dem Deutschen Volke" were added to the facade of this building, which is where Germany was officially reunified in 1990. In response to an event in this building, a decree was signed suspending civil liberties in the Weimar Republic. For 10 points, name this building that was set on fire in 1933, allowing Hitler to consolidate power.

tossups.question: D. Sefton Delmar’s primary account of an event at this building includes witnessing a man triumphantly proclaiming “This is a God-given signal!” A plan to drop banners on this building on May First failed when they fell through its ceiling. A February 28th decree limited freedom of the press and suspended habeas corpus in the wake of an event at this building. It was shown in the 1990s that (*) Yevgeny Khaldei took a photograph of a soldier raising a flag over its bombed husk. After an event in this building, Georgi Dimitrov was acquitted in a trial held in Leipzig, but Marinus van der Lubbe was pronounced guilty and beheaded. After the destruction of this building, a parliament was moved to the nearby Kroll Opera House. For 10 points, name this German seat of government that was set ablaze in 1933.
tossups.question_sanitized: D. Sefton Delmar's primary account of an event at this building includes witnessing a man triumphantly proclaiming "This is a God-given signal!" A plan to drop banners on this building on May First failed when they fell through its ceiling. A February 28th decree limited freedom of the press and suspended habeas corpus in the wake of an event at this building. It was shown in the 1990s that (*) Yevgeny Khaldei took a photograph of a soldier raising a flag over its bombed husk. After an event in this building, Georgi Dimitrov was acquitted in a trial held in Leipzig, but Marinus van der Lubbe was pronounced guilty and beheaded. After the destruction of this building, a parliament was moved to the nearby Kroll Opera House. For 10 points, name this German seat of government that was set ablaze in 1933.

tossups.question: The Manchester Guardian published two articles about this event based on a memorandum allegedly written by Ernst Oberfohren. Otto Katz and Willi Muenzenberg published an account of this event in the Brown Books that was largely discredited in articles in Der Spiegel in 1960 by amateur historian Fritz Tobias. Ernst Torgler was found not guilty for causing this event in the Leipzig Trial, which was followed by Soviet citizenship being granted to Blagoy Popov, Vasil Tanev, and Georgi Dimitrov, three Bulgarians put on trial for it. This event was committed by the Dutchman Marinus van der Lubbe, and it led to a namesake decree that was used by the Nazis to target Communists. For 10 points, name this 1933 act of arson on the legislative building of the Weimar Republic.
tossups.answer: Reichstag fire [accept equivalent answers such as burning of the Reichstag building]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Reichstag fire [accept equivalent answers such as burning of the Reichstag building]
tossups.question_sanitized: The Manchester Guardian published two articles about this event based on a memorandum allegedly written by Ernst Oberfohren. Otto Katz and Willi Muenzenberg published an account of this event in the Brown Books that was largely discredited in articles in Der Spiegel in 1960 by amateur historian Fritz Tobias. Ernst Torgler was found not guilty for causing this event in the Leipzig Trial, which was followed by Soviet citizenship being granted to Blagoy Popov, Vasil Tanev, and Georgi Dimitrov, three Bulgarians put on trial for it. This event was committed by the Dutchman Marinus van der Lubbe, and it led to a namesake decree that was used by the Nazis to target Communists. For 10 points, name this 1933 act of arson on the legislative building of the Weimar Republic.

tossups.question: An art installation in one of this building's courtyards consisted of neon lights forming a word in the middle of a box that was to be filled with dirt using sacks provided to people working in the building. The initial design for this building came from Paul Wallot, and above the entrance to this building is an inscription reading “To the People.” This building contains a cone-shaped light sculpture made of 360 mirrors that descends from the top of the building's glass (*) cupola. This building was renovated in the 1990s by a Norman Foster-led team. In 1995, Christo and Jean-Claude wrapped this building in polypropylene. Some of its walls are covered in graffiti left by Soviet soldiers. For 10 points, name this building where the Bundestag, the German Parliament, meets.
tossups.question_sanitized: An art installation in one of this building's courtyards consisted of neon lights forming a word in the middle of a box that was to be filled with dirt using sacks provided to people working in the building. The initial design for this building came from Paul Wallot, and above the entrance to this building is an inscription reading "To the People." This building contains a cone-shaped light sculpture made of 360 mirrors that descends from the top of the building's glass (*) cupola. This building was renovated in the 1990s by a Norman Foster-led team. In 1995, Christo and Jean-Claude wrapped this building in polypropylene. Some of its walls are covered in graffiti left by Soviet soldiers. For 10 points, name this building where the Bundestag, the German Parliament, meets.

bonuses.leadin: A session of this body which was called to handle unresolved matters from the Thirty Years’ War operated in perpetuity in Regensburg until 1806. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A session of this body which was called to handle unresolved matters from the Thirty Years' War operated in perpetuity in Regensburg until 1806. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this deliberative body whose permanent members included the Archbishops of Trier (“treer”) and Mainz. This body passed the Golden Bull of 1356, which set the size of the College of Electors to seven.
In 1623, this region’s Duke Maximillian I became the eighth elector to the Imperial Diet. This German state was historically ruled by Wittelsbach kings like Ludwig II.

This theologian proposed democratizing the election of the Holy Roman Emperor in his De Concordantia Theologica, written after he was excommunicated for opposing Pope Martin V’s selection of Archbishop of Trier.
parts_sanitized: Name this deliberative body whose permanent members included the Archbishops of Trier ("treer") and Mainz. This body passed the Golden Bull of 1356, which set the size of the College of Electors to seven.
In 1623, this region's Duke Maximillian I became the eighth elector to the Imperial Diet. This German state was historically ruled by Wittelsbach kings like Ludwig II.

This theologian proposed democratizing the election of the Holy Roman Emperor in his De Concordantia Theologica, written after he was excommunicated for opposing Pope Martin V's selection of Archbishop of Trier.
answers: Imperial Diet [or Reichstag; or Dieta Imperii; or Comitium Imperiale; or Diet of the Holy Roman Empire; accept the Eternal Diet of Regensburg or Immerwährender Reichstag; accept Diet of Metz]
Bavaria [or Bayern; accept the Kingdom of Bavaria; accept Duchy of Bavaria; accept Free State of Bavaria]
Nicholas of Cusa [or Nicholas of Kues; or Nicolas Cusanus; or Nicolas Cusanus]
answers_sanitized: Imperial Diet [or Reichstag; or Dieta Imperii; or Comitium Imperiale; or Diet of the Holy Roman Empire; accept the Eternal Diet of Regensburg or Immerwahrender Reichstag; accept Diet of Metz]
Bavaria [or Bayern; accept the Kingdom of Bavaria; accept Duchy of Bavaria; accept Free State of Bavaria]
Nicholas of Cusa [or Nicholas of Kues; or Nicolas Cusanus; or Nicolas Cusanus]

bonuses.leadin: A gilt eagle named for this person is mounted on a vase and displayed in the Louvre alongside the French crown jewels. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A gilt eagle named for this person is mounted on a vase and displayed in the Louvre alongside the French crown jewels. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this royal advisor who was convinced by Bernard of Clairvaux’s Apologia to devote himself to religious life. He later unsuccessfully lobbied Louis VII not to join the Second Crusade.
Suger (“soo-zhair”) briefly stayed at this pope’s court. Letters attributed to this pope appear in his namesake codex, which served as a guide for pilgrims on the Camino de Santiago.
Callixtus II ended the investiture controversy by signing a concordat with Henry V in this Rhenish city, where Charles V later issued an edict branding Martin Luther a heretic.
parts_sanitized: Name this royal advisor who was convinced by Bernard of Clairvaux's Apologia to devote himself to religious life. He later unsuccessfully lobbied Louis VII not to join the Second Crusade.
Suger ("soo-zhair") briefly stayed at this pope's court. Letters attributed to this pope appear in his namesake codex, which served as a guide for pilgrims on the Camino de Santiago.
Callixtus II ended the investiture controversy by signing a concordat with Henry V in this Rhenish city, where Charles V later issued an edict branding Martin Luther a heretic.
answers: Abbot Suger (“soo-zhair”) [or Abbé Suger; or Sugerius; accept Suger’s Eagle or Aigle du Suger]
Worms [or Free Imperial City of Worms or Freie Reichsstadt Worms; accept Concordat of Worms or Wormser Konkordat; accept Diet of Worms, Edict of Worms, Reichstag zu Worms, or Wormser Edikt]
answers_sanitized: Abbot Suger ("soo-zhair") [or Abbe Suger; or Sugerius; accept Suger's Eagle or Aigle du Suger]

packet.name: F. Prelims 6 - Minnesota B, Ottawa A, Rutgers A, Vanderbilt A

bonuses.leadin: Despite the fact that initially only he received credit for their work, they flew in separate planes so the other could complete their work in case one died. For 10 points each, answer some questions about Christo and Jeanne-Claude.
parts: A series of works by Christo and Jeanne-Claude involve wrapping things in various fabrics. They’ve done this to landmarks such as the Pont-neuf and this German parliament building, whose glass dome was designed by Norman Foster.

Another project by Christo and Jeanne-Claude was the installation of thousands of these objects in Japan and California. 1,340 blue ones were installed in Japan while 1,760 yellow ones were installed in California. They had to removed when one toppled and killed a person.

For the artwork The Gates, Christo and Jeanne-Claude installed over 7,500 vinyl gates in this city. Other installation pieces in this city include Richard Serra’s Tilted Arc and a room containing over 250 cubic yards of dirt.
answers_sanitized: Reichstag
Umbrellas
New York
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Despite the fact that initially only he received credit for their work, they flew in separate planes so the other could complete their work in case one died. For 10 points each, answer some questions about Christo and Jeanne-Claude.
parts_sanitized: A series of works by Christo and Jeanne-Claude involve wrapping things in various fabrics. They've done this to landmarks such as the Pont-neuf and this German parliament building, whose glass dome was designed by Norman Foster.

Another project by Christo and Jeanne-Claude was the installation of thousands of these objects in Japan and California. 1,340 blue ones were installed in Japan while 1,760 yellow ones were installed in California. They had to removed when one toppled and killed a person.

For the artwork The Gates, Christo and Jeanne-Claude installed over 7,500 vinyl gates in this city. Other installation pieces in this city include Richard Serra's Tilted Arc and a room containing over 250 cubic yards of dirt.

bonuses.leadin: James Bradley wrote Flags of Our Fathers about the soldiers depicted in this image, though in 2016 it was discovered that his father John Bradley had been misidentified. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this iconic photograph taken by Joe Rosenthal during a 1945 battle on a Japanese island. Felix de Weldon later used this image to sculpt the Marine Corps War Memorial.

Yevgeny Khaldei’s 1945 photograph of this building depicts a Soviet soldier hoisting the Hammer and Sickle over it during the Battle of Berlin. This building was severely damaged by a 1933 arson attack.
A photograph from this war depicts a Royal Marine “yomper” with the Union Jack flying from his pack on a march from the San Carlos beachhead.
answers_sanitized: Raising the Flag on Iwo Jima
Reichstag (RYKE-shtogg)
Falklands War [or Guerra de las Malvinas]

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about Joseph Stalin literally taking people and objects out of the picture, for 10 points each.
parts: The Great Purge is sometimes named for this man, who headed the NKVD as the People’s Commissar for Internal Affairs from 1936 to 1938. After his 1940 execution, he himself was purged from a picture taken at the Moscow Canal.

With his brother-in-law, Lev Kamenev, this man was edited out of a photograph of Lenin giving a 1920 speech. The reach of the USSR finally caught up to him when he was murdered, most likely with an axe, in Mexico City.

A very slight change removed Abdulkhakim Ismailov’s wristwatch from a picture of this action. Stalin pressured troops to fight harder so that a picture of this action could be taken on International Workers’ Day, 1945. They were one day late.
answers: raising a flag over the Reichstag [prompt on: “Battle of Berlin”, “fall of Germany”, similar answers]
answers_sanitized: Nikolai Yezhov
Leon Trotsky [or Lev Davidovich Bronshtein]
raising a flag over the Reichstag [prompt on: "Battle of Berlin", "fall of Germany", similar answers]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about Joseph Stalin literally taking people and objects out of the picture, for 10 points each.
parts_sanitized: The Great Purge is sometimes named for this man, who headed the NKVD as the People's Commissar for Internal Affairs from 1936 to 1938. After his 1940 execution, he himself was purged from a picture taken at the Moscow Canal.

With his brother-in-law, Lev Kamenev, this man was edited out of a photograph of Lenin giving a 1920 speech. The reach of the USSR finally caught up to him when he was murdered, most likely with an axe, in Mexico City.

A very slight change removed Abdulkhakim Ismailov's wristwatch from a picture of this action. Stalin pressured troops to fight harder so that a picture of this action could be taken on International Workers' Day, 1945. They were one day late.

bonuses.leadin: David King wrote an authoritative study of this practice, whose most famous target may have been the water commissar Nikolai Yeshov. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this practice that Soviet leaders such as Stalin carried out in order to make it appear that deceased dissidents never existed.

This lost cosmonaut, who suffered third-degree burns when he foolishly tried to use his coveralls to smother an ignited alcohol-soaked cotton ball in a high oxygen altitude chamber, was airbrushed out of a group photograph of the first six cosmonauts.
In a much less horrific instance of airbrushing, photographer Yevgeny Khaldei removed the watch from the wrist of a soldier performing this action after the Battle of Berlin.
answers: airbrushing photos [accept descriptive answers indicating that they were removed from photos]
answers_sanitized: airbrushing photos [accept descriptive answers indicating that they were removed from photos]
Valentin Bondarenko [or Valentin Vasiliyevich Bondarenko]
raising a flag on the Reichstag
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: David King wrote an authoritative study of this practice, whose most famous target may have been the water commissar Nikolai Yeshov. For 10 points each:

In a much less horrific instance of airbrushing, photographer Yevgeny Khaldei removed the watch from the wrist of a soldier performing this action after the Battle of Berlin.

bonuses.leadin: Germany lost both World Wars, but the time between those wars wasn’t too great for it either. Answer some questions about that period in German history. For 10 points each:
parts: This government was created after the First World War in the namesake town. In power until the rise of the Third Reich, it had to deal with hyperinflation and the payment of $5 billion in war reparations.

This first president of the Weimar Republic was Germany’s first democratically elected leader. This Social Democrat signed a pact with Army Quartermaster General Wilhelm Groener and fled Berlin during the Kapp Putsch.
This February 1933 event occurred six days before Parliamentary elections. Allegedly set by Marinus van der Lubbe, the Nazi party blamed this event on the Communists.
answers_sanitized: Weimar Republic (prompt on "German Reich")
Friedrich Ebert
burning of the Reichstag (accept equivalents like "fire" and "arson")
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Germany lost both World Wars, but the time between those wars wasn't too great for it either. Answer some questions about that period in German history. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: This government was created after the First World War in the namesake town. In power until the rise of the Third Reich, it had to deal with hyperinflation and the payment of $5 billion in war reparations.

This first president of the Weimar Republic was Germany's first democratically elected leader. This Social Democrat signed a pact with Army Quartermaster General Wilhelm Groener and fled Berlin during the Kapp Putsch.
This February 1933 event occurred six days before Parliamentary elections. Allegedly set by Marinus van der Lubbe, the Nazi party blamed this event on the Communists.

bonuses.leadin: After his wife died in a riding accident, this man schemed for decades to ensure a good succession for his son Philip the Handsome, who ended up getting hitched to Joanna the Mad. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Holy Roman Emperor who married his daughter away to the future Charles VIII of France in the Treaty of Arras.

In 1495 at Worms, Maximilian agreed to judicial and tax changes passed through the Imperial Diet, which was also known by this name. This historical name for the German Parliament was replaced in 1949 by the Bundestag.
Maximilian's troops lost the Battle of Dornach during a war with this power, whose aims at expansion would be stopped at the Battle of Marignano.
answers_sanitized: Maximilian I Habsburg
Reichstag
Switzerland [or Schweiz; or Suisse; or the Old Swiss Confederacy]

bonuses.leadin: Answer stuff about the decrees of Nazi Germany, FTPE.
parts: Paul von Hindenburg issued a decree "for the Protection of People and State" after this 1933 event, curtailing freedom of press and suspending political rights. Dutch communist Marinus van der Lubbe was convicted of causing this event.

The Nazis followed their gain of power in the Reichstag Fire Decree by pushing for this act, which authorized Hitler to issue decrees without consent from the president or the Reichstag, effectively granting him dictatorship.

Hitler issued this secret decree on December 7, 1941, authorizing the murder or forced disappearance of political enemies of the Nazis. Wilhelm Keitel's signature of this act was a main factor in his death sentence at Nuremburg.
answers: Enabling Act of 1933 [or Ermächtigungsgesetz; or Gesetz zur Behebung der Not von Volk und Reich; accept "Law for Removing the Distress of the People and the Reich" or other reasonable translations]
answers_sanitized: Reichstag fire
Enabling Act of 1933 [or Ermachtigungsgesetz; or Gesetz zur Behebung der Not von Volk und Reich; accept "Law for Removing the Distress of the People and the Reich" or other reasonable translations]
Night and Fog Decree [or Nacht-und-Nebel-Erlass]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer stuff about the decrees of Nazi Germany, FTPE.
parts_sanitized: Paul von Hindenburg issued a decree "for the Protection of People and State" after this 1933 event, curtailing freedom of press and suspending political rights. Dutch communist Marinus van der Lubbe was convicted of causing this event.

The Nazis followed their gain of power in the Reichstag Fire Decree by pushing for this act, which authorized Hitler to issue decrees without consent from the president or the Reichstag, effectively granting him dictatorship.

Hitler issued this secret decree on December 7, 1941, authorizing the murder or forced disappearance of political enemies of the Nazis. Wilhelm Keitel's signature of this act was a main factor in his death sentence at Nuremburg.
